summary: Ukrainian strike drones caused a power outage in Sevastopol, leaving the city without electricity. Ukrainian special forces hit military airfields and air defence assets in occupied Crimea, damaging aircraft hangars and S‑400 systems. Russian UAV activity continues across multiple front‑line regions.
key_points: Ukrainian drones overloaded air defenses and knocked out power in Sevastopol, the hero city, after a carpet‑style UAV attack (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian Alpha special forces struck the Sakky and Hvardiyiske airfields and damaged four aircraft hangars, two S‑400 units and two Pantsir‑S1 systems in Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
A Russian UAV attacked a fuel station on the outskirts of Sumy, injuring three civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82145).
Four groups of UAVs were observed heading toward Shostka in Sumy region from the north (Source: @eRadarrua/98011).
Ukrainian operators of the 291st Motorized Rifle Regiment are conducting anti‑sniper operations in the Zaporizhzhia area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31119).
Russian forces reportedly liberated the village of Ivolzhanskoye in Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31132).
Ukrainian‑made "Vyravnyvatel" guided aerial bombs, 250 kg, are now in production and can be launched from 10‑140 km (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125).
Ukrainian UAVs were sighted moving eastward in Dnipropetrovsk toward Shakhtarske and Pavlohrad, and northward in Zaporizhzhia toward Novomykolaivka (Source: @kpszsu/66234).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian UAV attacks have knocked out power in Sevastopol, highlighting a successful saturation of Russian air defenses. Simultaneously, Ukrainian special forces struck key airfield infrastructure and air‑defence assets in occupied Crimea, damaging hangars and S‑400 systems. Russian UAV activity continues across the front, with multiple sightings in Sumy, Zaporizhzhia and Dnipropetrovsk regions.

points: Ukrainian operators of the 291st Motorized Rifle Regiment are conducting anti‑sniper operations in the Zaporizhzhia area, targeting Ukrainian snipers in cellars (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31119).

The Ukrainian Armed Forces have begun fielding the domestically produced "Vyravnyvatel" guided aerial bomb, a 250 kg weapon with a launch range of 10‑140 km, though high‑altitude release is limited by Russian air defenses (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125).

Ukrainian Alpha special forces hit the Sakky and Hvardiyiske airfields in Crimea, destroying four aircraft hangars and damaging two S‑400 and two Pantsir‑S1 air‑defence units (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed moving eastward in Dnipropetrovsk toward Shakhtarske and Pavlohrad, and northward in Zaporizhzhia toward Novomykolaivka (Source: @kpszsu/66234).
Four groups of UAVs were reported heading toward Shostka in Sumy region from the north, indicating coordinated reconnaissance or strike missions (Source: @eRadarrua/98011).
Ukrainian forces reported a rapid UAV strike that overloaded Russian air‑defence capacity, resulting in a city‑wide power outage in Sevastopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian troops continue to engage Russian forces on the Lyman, Kupiansk, South‑Slobozhansky and North‑Slobozhansky directions, noting increased Russian activity (Source: @UkraineNow/82138).
Russian authorities announced that convicted foreign agents will serve sentences of three to five years upon return to Russia (Source: @tass_agency/382223).
President Putin called for accelerated development of Russian aviation, including increased production of MC‑21, SSJ‑100 and Il‑114 aircraft (Source: @tass_agency/382246).
Russian officials warned of heightened legal risks for unregistered marriages, framing them as a national security threat (Source: @tass_agency/382219).
Russia prepared legal measures to protect assets abroad in case of foreign seizure, as stated by Medvedev (Source: @tass_agency/382239).
A Russian court seized the property of former Moscow City Duma deputy Kruglov for spreading false information about the Russian Armed Forces (Source: @tass_agency/382241).
Russian air defence forces reported shooting down 323 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions, with two civilian casualties (Source: @tass_agency/382221).
Russian forces claimed liberation of the village Ivolzhanskoye in Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31132).
Ukrainian UAVs were sighted moving eastward in Dnipropetrovsk toward Shakhtarske and Pavlohrad, indicating pressure on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/66234).
Four groups of UAVs were observed heading toward Shostka in Sumy, suggesting intensified activity on the northern part of the eastern front (Source: @eRadarrua/98011).
Ukrainian Alpha special forces struck airfields and air‑defence assets in occupied Crimea, affecting Russian rear‑area capabilities on the eastern flank (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
Russian troops reportedly liberated Ivolzhanskoye in Sumy, indicating a localized counter‑offensive on the eastern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31132).
Ukrainian operators of the 291st Motorized Rifle Regiment are conducting anti‑sniper operations in Zaporizhzhia, a key eastern sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31119).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported near Bogoduhiv in Kharkiv region, showing continued aerial activity in the east (Source: @eRadarrua/97994).
Russian "Geranium" drones reportedly hit railway trains carrying Ukrainian military supplies in Kharkiv region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed north of Kharkiv, near Bogoduhiv, indicating reconnaissance or strike missions on the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/97994).
Four UAV groups heading toward Shostka from the north suggest heightened activity on the Sumy northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/98011).
Ukrainian Alpha forces damaged air‑defence assets in Crimea, which supports Russian operations on the northern front (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
Russian legal actions against foreign agents and unregistered marriages may affect internal security dynamics on the northern front (Source: @tass_agency/382223, @tass_agency/382219).
Ukrainian anti‑sniper operations in Zaporizhzhia could have spill‑over effects toward the northern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31119).
Russian forces reported shooting down 323 UAVs across Russian regions, reflecting intense aerial engagements that may involve the northern sector (Source: @tass_agency/382221).
Ukrainian UAV activity near Sumy and Shostka indicates ongoing pressure on Russian positions in the north (Source: @eRadarrua/98011).
A Russian UAV attacked a fuel station on the outskirts of Sumy, injuring three civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82145).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported moving eastward in Dnipropetrovsk toward Shakhtarske and Pavlohrad, affecting the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/66234).
Ukrainian forces reported a large‑scale offensive campaign across multiple directions, including the Lyman and South‑Slobozhansky axes (Source: @UkraineNow/82138).
Russian air‑defence units in Crimea were hit, potentially reducing Russian air‑cover for southern operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
Ukrainian "Vyravnyvatel" guided bombs are now in production, offering new strike capabilities for southern operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125).
Russian authorities warned of increased legal penalties for foreign agents, potentially affecting support networks in the south (Source: @tass_agency/382223).
Ukrainian Alpha special forces damaged aircraft hangars in Crimea, which could impact Russian air support for southern fronts (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
Ukrainian UAV saturation caused a power outage in Sevastopol after overwhelming Russian air‑defence systems (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Russian air‑defence reported shooting down 323 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory, with two civilian casualties (Source: @tass_agency/382221).
Ukrainian Alpha forces struck airfields and S‑400 systems in Crimea, degrading Russian air‑defence capabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
Russian President Putin called for accelerated production of MC‑21, SSJ‑100 and Il‑114 aircraft to strengthen domestic aviation (Source: @tass_agency/382246).
Ukrainian "Vyravnyvatel" guided aerial bombs, 250 kg, now in production, can be launched from 10‑140 km, expanding Ukraine's strike envelope (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125).
Russian "Geranium" drones reportedly hit railway trains carrying Ukrainian military supplies in Kharkiv, indicating Russian UAV offensive use (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed moving toward multiple front‑line cities, including Zaporizhzhia, Dnipro and Kharkiv, showing broad aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/66234, @eRadarrua/97994).

key_developments: Ukrainian UAVs overloaded Russian air‑defences, causing a city‑wide power outage in Sevastopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian Alpha special forces damaged four aircraft hangars and S‑400/Pantsir‑S1 systems at Sakky and Hvardiyiske airfields in Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/82144).
Russian air‑defence forces reported downing 323 Ukrainian UAVs across Russian regions, with two civilian injuries (Source: @tass_agency/382221).
Four groups of UAVs were observed heading toward Shostka in Sumy region, indicating coordinated strike activity (Source: @eRadarrua/98011).
Ukrainian "Vyravnyvatel" guided aerial bombs entered production, offering 250 kg payloads at 10‑140 km range (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125).
Russian President Putin urged faster, larger‑scale development of domestic aircraft, including MC‑21 and SSJ‑100 (Source: @tass_agency/382246).
Russian forces claimed liberation of Ivolzhanskoye village in Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31132).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV operations have demonstrated the ability to saturate and temporarily disable Russian air‑defence networks, as seen in Sevastopol. Russian air‑defence remains active, claiming high UAV kill counts, but losses of critical air‑defence assets in Crimea reduce its protective depth. The introduction of the "Vyravnyvatel" guided bomb expands Ukraine's stand‑off strike capability, potentially increasing pressure on Russian rear areas.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is intensifying UAV deployments across multiple fronts, targeting logistics hubs, airfields and air‑defence sites to degrade Russian operational capacity. Russia is responding with accelerated aircraft production programs and legal measures to tighten internal security, while continuing to claim high UAV interception rates. International actors are monitoring the escalation, with EU officials discussing deeper defence integration for Ukraine.
sections.intelligence_notes: Future Ukrainian UAV missions may focus on further saturation of Russian air‑defence corridors, especially around strategic ports and power infrastructure. Russian legal actions against foreign agents and unregistered marriages suggest a tightening of internal security that could affect foreign support networks. Monitoring the deployment of "Vyravnyvatel" bombs will be critical to assess changes in Ukrainian strike depth and potential impacts on Russian rear‑area logistics.
